epic.title: Apps (Flutter): client & provider
epic.summary: Two Flutter apps over the shared .NET API: a client app (book, intake/consent, photos, memberships, rewards, balances, card-on-file) and a provider app (room-side charting, injection mapping, camera capture, consult/Rx, finalise) — the latter resilient to treatment-room connectivity. One codebase, two flavours, sharing auth, the API client and the design system.

stories.title: Client app: book → intake → consent journey
stories.context: A client can complete the full pre-visit journey entirely in-app (REQ-APP-1).
stories.i_want: to book, complete intake and e-sign consent entirely in the app
stories.so_that: I'm ready for my visit without paperwork
acceptance: A client completes book → intake → consent (incl. image-use) fully in-app.
Surfaces PRD-02 booking and PRD-03 intake/consent.
Sign-in via social/email/OTP, tenant-scoped.
Reminders/recall (PRD-07) are received in-app.
detail: The client completes the full pre-visit journey entirely in-app: book -> intake -> e-sign consent (incl. image-use), then receives reminders/recall. Surfaces PRD-02 booking and PRD-03 intake/consent through the Flutter client app; sign-in via social/email/OTP, tenant-scoped.
Removes paperwork and gets clients ready before they arrive.
ui_spec: Prototype: client app (client-app.png) bottom-tabs — Home/For you, Book, then the intake + consent flow ('You're booked!', 'All set — thank you').

data_model.notes: Client app is a surface over those modules.

stories.title: Client app: my care, memberships, rewards & card-on-file
stories.context: Clients view consented before/after photos, memberships, rewards/perks and balances, and add a card-on-file for autopay (REQ-APP-1).
stories.i_want: to view my photos, memberships, rewards and balance and add a card-on-file
stories.so_that: I can self-serve my care and payments
acceptance: Consent-gated before/after photo viewing.
Memberships, rewards/perks and balances visible.
Card-on-file can be added in-app (feeds PRD-06 autopay).
No one-off online checkout is exposed.
detail: In the app the client views consented before/after photos, memberships, rewards/perks and balances, and adds a card-on-file for autopay (feeds PRD-06). No one-off online checkout is exposed.
Self-service for care history and payments.
ui_spec: Prototype: client app (client-app.png) tabs — My care (visit history, photos with consent), Rewards (redeem points, gift cards), Account (update card on file).

data_model.notes: Card-on-file feeds PRD-06 autopay; no one-off checkout.

stories.title: Client app: account, privacy & access/correction
stories.context: The Account area exposes profile, balances, card-on-file and a 'Your data & privacy' surface (residency note, access copy, request correction) (C21).
stories.i_want: an account area where I can see my data, know it stays in Australia, and request access or correction
stories.so_that: I'm in control of my information
acceptance: Account shows profile, balances and card-on-file.
'Your data & privacy' shows the residency note and access-copy/correction actions (PRD-01).
Requests are tracked to resolution.
Actions are audited.
detail: The Account area exposes profile, balances, card-on-file and a 'Your data & privacy' surface: the residency note ('your data stays in Australia'), request a copy, request correction, and request account deletion — tracked to resolution and audited (APP 12/13, PRD-01).
Puts the client in control of their information.
ui_spec: Prototype: client app Account (client-app.png) — 'Edit profile', 'Update card on file', 'Request account deletion', privacy/access-correction; residency trust note.

data_model.notes: Self-service entry point; staff resolve via Governance.

stories.title: Provider app: day schedule & open patient
stories.context: The provider sees their day and opens a patient with consult+consent status verified before charting (REQ-APP-2).
stories.i_want: to see my day and open a patient with consult/consent status shown before I chart
stories.so_that: I work room-side with the right context and gates
acceptance: Schedule shows the practitioner's day; opening a patient shows consult+consent status.
Provider signs in via Entra SSO, tenant-scoped.
The consult+consent gate (PRD-03/04) is enforced before charting opens.
Quick links to map/photos/finalise.
detail: The provider app shows the practitioner's day and opens a patient with consult+consent status shown before charting — the consult+consent gate (PRD-03/04) is enforced before charting opens. Provider signs in via Entra SSO, tenant-scoped.
Room-side starting point with the right context and gates.
ui_spec: Prototype: provider app (treatment-room.png) — 'Today · Room 2' schedule; open a patient -> 'Consent & prescriber authorisation' status; quick links to map/photos/finalise.
Bottom tabs: Schedule / Patient / Medicines / Tasks.

stories.title: Provider app: room-side charting, camera & finalise
stories.context: Map injections, capture photos via signed URLs (never on device), record consult/link script and finalise — all surfacing PRD-04/05 (REQ-APP-2, C14/ADR-0009).
stories.i_want: to map injections, capture photos and finalise the chart room-side
stories.so_that: the full clinical record is captured at the chair
acceptance: Provider app surfaces PRD-05 mapping/photos and PRD-04 consult/Rx/administration.
Photos capture to central storage via signed URLs; none persist on device after sync (C14).
Finalisation is server-side; once finalised the entry is read-only.
Thumb-first, gloves-on usability (UX §1).
detail: Room-side charting on the provider app: map injections, capture photos via signed URLs (never on device), record consult/link script and finalise — surfacing PRD-04/05. Thumb-first, gloves-on usability; finalisation is server-side and the entry becomes read-only once final.
The hero clinical workflow at the chair.
ui_spec: Prototype: provider app (treatment-room.png) — 'Treatment record', 'Treatment settings', the injection map + camera; finalise locks the note.
Photos capture to central storage; none persist on device after sync (C14).

stories.title: Provider app: offline-tolerant workflows + sync indicator
stories.context: Charting/photos queue locally encrypted and sync on reconnect with no loss; a persistent sync/offline indicator shows queued count + last sync (REQ-APP-3, ADR-0015).
stories.i_want: the app to keep working offline and clearly show my sync state
stories.so_that: treatment-room Wi-Fi drops never cost me data
acceptance: Charting/photos queue locally (encrypted) offline and sync on reconnect with no loss.
Persistent indicator shows queued-items count + last-sync time.
Finalise is disabled until synced.
Built on SPIKE-OFFLINE and PRD-05/OFFLINE.
detail: The provider app keeps working offline: charting/photos queue locally (encrypted) and sync on reconnect with no loss; a persistent indicator shows queued count + last-sync, and finalise is disabled until synced (ADR-0015, built on SPIKE-OFFLINE + PRD-05/OFFLINE).
Treatment-room Wi-Fi drops never cost the clinician data.
ui_spec: Prototype: provider app (treatment-room.png) — a persistent sync/offline indicator; queued-items count + last-sync; finalise disabled until synced.

stories.title: App distribution & code-push posture
stories.context: Store distribution + code-push (e.g. Shorebird) where the compliance posture allows (open question) (ADR-0006).
stories.i_want: a store-distribution and (where viable) code-push pipeline for both apps
stories.so_that: we can ship and patch the apps responsibly
acceptance: Both apps distribute via internal/store channels from CI.
Code-push viability for the compliance posture is assessed and documented.
Versioning + minimum-supported-version handling in place.
Crash/usage telemetry feeds observability.
detail: Store distribution + (where the compliance posture allows) code-push (e.g. Shorebird) for both Flutter apps from CI, with versioning + minimum-supported-version handling and crash/usage telemetry into observability (ADR-0006).
Lets the clinic ship and responsibly patch the apps.
ui_spec: No in-app screen — release pipeline + an in-app 'update required' gate for unsupported versions.

stories.title: Reception self-check-in surface (tablet)
stories.context: The prototype's checkin surface is a reception-desk tablet where clients check themselves in (confirm details, complete any outstanding intake/consent), updating the Today board.
stories.i_want: to check myself in on the reception tablet
stories.so_that: the team knows I've arrived and I complete anything outstanding
acceptance: A client can self-check-in (verify identity/appointment) on a shared tablet surface.
Outstanding intake/consent is prompted and completable at check-in.
Check-in updates the visit lifecycle/Today board (PRD-02).
The surface is locked-down (no access to other clients' data) and times out between clients.
detail: A reception-desk tablet where clients self-check-in: verify identity/appointment and complete any outstanding intake/consent, updating the visit lifecycle / Today board (PRD-02). The surface is locked-down (no access to other clients' data) and times out between clients.
Speeds arrivals and captures missing pre-visit items.
ui_spec: Prototype: check-in tablet (checkin.png) — 'Welcome in' self-check-in; verify appointment, complete outstanding intake/consent; returns to a neutral state between clients.

stories.title: Back-office / bench tablet surface
stories.context: The prototype's backroom surface is a bench tablet for behind-the-scenes work: open/close, cold-chain, stock on hand, S4 drug register, waste/disposal log, tasks and shift handover — a focused operations view.
stories.i_want: a bench-tablet view of the day's operational jobs
stories.so_that: behind-the-scenes work (logs, stock, handover) is quick at the bench
acceptance: Surfaces open/close checklist, cold-chain log, stock on hand, S4 register, waste log, tasks and shift handover.
Each panel reuses the underlying modules (PRD-04 medicines, PRD-11 operations, PRD-07 jobs).
Role/financial gating applies; actions are audited.
Usable on a shared device with appropriate session handling.
detail: A bench tablet for behind-the-scenes work: open/close checklist, cold-chain log, stock on hand, S4 drug register, waste/disposal log, tasks and shift handover — each panel reusing the underlying modules (PRD-04 medicines, PRD-11 operations, PRD-07 jobs). Role/financial gating applies; actions audited.
A focused operations view for the back room.
ui_spec: Prototype: back-office tablet (backroom.png) — 'Good morning', Open & close, Cold chain, Stock on hand, Schedule 4 drug register, Waste & disposal log, Tasks, Shift handover.

stories.title: Client 'report a concern' → follow-up / AE bridge
stories.context: The prototype's client app lets a client report a post-treatment concern, which bridges into staff follow-ups (and can escalate to an adverse event/complaint). A safety-critical client→clinic channel.
stories.i_want: to report a concern after my treatment from the app
stories.so_that: the clinic responds quickly if something's wrong
acceptance: A client can submit a concern (with optional photo, consent-respecting) from the app.
The concern raises a follow-up job for staff (PRD-07) with the client/treatment linked.
Staff can call back, resolve, or escalate to an adverse event (PRD-05) / complaint (PRD-11).
The client sees acknowledgement; the exchange is recorded and audited.
detail: A safety-critical client->clinic channel: the client reports a post-treatment concern (with optional photo, consent-respecting) from the app, raising a follow-up job for staff with the client/treatment linked. Staff can call back, resolve, or escalate to an adverse event (PRD-05) / complaint (PRD-11). The client sees acknowledgement; the exchange is recorded and audited.
Bridges the client app to staff follow-ups (the concern bridge, localStorage in the proto).
ui_spec: Prototype: client app 'Report a concern' (client-app.png) -> appears in staff Follow-ups (followups.png) as a job (openConcern/concernCall).

data_model.fields: id, tenant_id, client_id, treatment_ref, body, photo_ref?, status, raised_at
